Ntc Of Motion For Order Compelling Responses, Without Objections To Request For Admissions And Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Jan-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. MITCHELL L. ABDALLAH OF THE ABDALLAH LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. (916) 446-1974 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. DAVID CHAVEZ OF ALVARADO SMITH, TEL. 624-8665 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CHASE BANK.
RULING - A R G U E D; THE PARTIES STIPULATED THAT PRO-TEM JUDGE ERNST HALPERIN CAN HEAR THIS MATTER. PLAINTIFF MICHEL RANTISSI'S Motion For Order Compelling Responses, Without Objections To Request For Admissions And Form Interrogatories is GRANTED. Defendant Washington Mutual Bank is ordered to serve complete responses, without objections, to Plaintiff's Requests for Admission - Set One, and Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories - Set One within 30 days of entry of this Order. Plaintiff's counsel's declaration states that he never received responses to the propounded discovery. The proofs of service on all of the discovery responses submitted for the Court's consideration as Exhibits 2 and 3 to the Declaration of David L. Chavez in Support of Opposition to Motion for an Order Compelling Responses show that the responses were not mailed to Plaintiff's counsel's address. They were mailed to "Abdallah Law Group, 1006 4th Street, 4th Floor, Sacramento, CA 94814." Plaintiff's counsel's address on both discovery requests was listed as "Abdallah Law Group, P.C., 555 Capitol Mall, Suite 725, Sacramento, CA 95814." That is also the address of record for Abdallah Law Group in the Court's Register of Actions for this case. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. The Court is unable to ascertain from the declaration of Plaintiff's counsel how many hours Plaintiff's counsel has spent on the matter and how much time his staff has spent on the matter.
